In her words

Not about hype.
About resilience, profit & long-term value.

"Over 14+ years I've built four companies — learning, sometimes the hard way, that real businesses aren't built on hype."

I'm a serial entrepreneur, builder and mother of three based in Asia, after living and working across three continents.

As CEO and co-founder of Nino Nana, I'm building Singapore's #1 independent baby diaper brand on a simple conviction: performance, non-toxic materials and environmental responsibility don't have to be a trade-off.

We're growing across markets and actively looking to partner with aligned distributors — anywhere in the world. If you're in FMCG and believe in better-for-you products, I'd love to talk.

I'm building alongside three kids — and trying to make my network a little less male-heavy along the way.

The journey

A track record of building.

From the world's largest youth-run organisation to founding consumer brands across Asia and Europe.

My first real leadership test was at AIESEC, the world’s largest youth-run organisation. I started out running it in Russia as President, then took on 26 countries across Europe and Central Asia as Regional Director, with around 13,000 people in the network. I was in my twenties, learning how to build teams and raise money as I went. It stuck.

A spell in management consulting at ACG Global scratched the itch for a while, but I really wanted to build something of my own. My first try, EvenPanda, didn’t fly, so I reshaped it into Clubvivre, an on-demand private chef service that grew into Singapore’s #1 and somehow ended up on TV, radio and in print.

Later, at Eco Trade International, I brought premium mother & baby brands into markets across Eastern Europe and Central Asia. Different places, different products, but the same lesson kept showing up: it’s the unglamorous fundamentals that actually build a company.

Open to partners

Bring Nino Nana to your market.

We're growing across markets and looking for aligned distributors who share a belief in better-for-you products. If you're in FMCG, let's talk.
